response: {"purpose":"Figure 3 evaluates the experimental scalability, reproducibility, and data quality of the eCLIP method across a large panel of RNA-binding protein (RBP) target identification experiments. Panel (a) demonstrates the throughput and success rate of 209 eCLIP experiments in two cell lines (K562 and HepG2), while panel (b) assesses the consistency of library amplification efficiency between biological replicates, with IgG-only controls providing negative reference points across a range of molecular weights. Panel (c) benchmarks eCLIP against existing CLIP-based methods (iCLIP, PAR-CLIP, HITS-CLIP) by comparing the fraction of non-PCR-duplicated usable reads, establishing that eCLIP produces higher-quality sequencing data. Together, these analyses demonstrate that eCLIP is both scalable to hundreds of experiments and superior in data yield and reproducibility relative to alternative CLIP approaches, justifying its adoption as a platform for systematic RBP characterization.","title":"Scalable eCLIP Enables High-Throughput RBP Target Identification Across Cell Lines"}
